report:
## Profitability

Across the three companies, KO stands out for its superior margin profile despite being the smallest by revenue, while PEP operates at a larger scale with moderately high margins, and COST runs a high-volume, razor-thin-margin warehouse model. KO grew revenues from $43.0B in FY2022 [KO:Q0] to $47.9B in FY2025 [KO:Q3], while delivering a net margin that expanded sharply to roughly 27% in FY2025 (net income $13.1B [KO:Q7] on revenues of $47.9B [KO:Q3]) and gross margins consistently above 60% (gross profit $29.5B in FY2025 [KO:Q15]). PEP operates at roughly double KO's revenue scale ($93.9B in FY2025 [PEP:Q3]) but saw net income decline to $8.2B in FY2025 [PEP:Q7] from a peak of $9.6B in FY2024 [PEP:Q6], and operating income slipped to $11.5B [PEP:Q11] from $12.9B [PEP:Q10], signaling margin pressure. COST dwarfs both in revenue ($275.2B in FY2025 [COST:Q3]) but its business model produces structurally thin margins; nonetheless, it has shown the strongest absolute earnings growth trajectory, with net income rising from $5.8B in FY2022 [COST:Q4] to $8.1B in FY2025 [COST:Q7] and operating income climbing from $7.8B [COST:Q8] to $10.4B [COST:Q11], reflecting consistent operational leverage. In summary, KO leads on margin quality, COST leads on earnings growth momentum, and PEP faces the most near-term profitability headwinds.

**KO:** Coca-Cola demonstrates the strongest margin profile of the three, with gross profit reaching $29.5B on $47.9B in revenues in FY2025 [KO:Q15][KO:Q3], implying a gross margin above 60%. Net income surged to $13.1B in FY2025 [KO:Q7], up from $10.6B in FY2024 [KO:Q6], and operating income recovered strongly to $13.8B [KO:Q11] after a dip to $10.0B in FY2024 [KO:Q10], reflecting KO's durable pricing power and high-margin concentrate business.

**PEP:** PepsiCo generates the largest absolute gross profit among the three ($50.9B in FY2025 [PEP:Q15]) on revenues of $93.9B [PEP:Q3], but profitability deteriorated in FY2025 as net income fell to $8.2B [PEP:Q7] from $9.6B in FY2024 [PEP:Q6] and operating income declined to $11.5B [PEP:Q11] from $12.9B [PEP:Q10], suggesting cost pressures or one-time charges are weighing on the bottom line.

**COST:** Costco's warehouse membership model produces structurally thin net margins (roughly 2.9% in FY2025 on $275.2B in revenues [COST:Q3][COST:Q7]), but the company has delivered consistent earnings growth, with net income rising to $8.1B in FY2025 [COST:Q7] from $5.8B in FY2022 [COST:Q4] and operating income expanding to $10.4B [COST:Q11] from $7.8B [COST:Q8], demonstrating strong operational leverage as membership and volume scale up.
